repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Begins as a memoir containing reminiscences of Lovejoy's boyhood, adolescence and young adulthood including memories of family, the town of Benton, Methodist Episcopal church members and activities including revivals and personal spiritual growth. Diary entries (1-5 per year) record activities and opinions as a religious leader in the Methodist Episcopal church; births and deaths of family members and friends; quarter meetings and district conferences attended; sermons heard and prayers for spiritual growth.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Peter Lovejoy was born February 18, 1805 in Kinderhook, Columbia County New York. In 1817 his family moved to the town of Ben ton, Yates County, New York (then Ontario County) as one of the earliest settlers in the area. In August, 1825, Lovejoy experienced a religious conversion and shortly afterward joined the Methodist Episcopal Church of Benton at Benton Centre. Although his trade was "clothdressing," Lovejoy's life revolved around his family and his church. In 1833 he was appointed to his first official position in the church as a class leader, a position he filled for 52 consecutive years, as well as serving in the positions of sexton, steward and trustee at various times throughout his membership in the church. He died on June 8, 1889, as the oldest member of the church.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The diary of Peter Lovejoy (1867-1878) begins as a memoir of his childhood, adolescent, and early adulthood religious experiences to 1867, when the diary entries begin. The memoir portion of the diary includes memories of family, the town of Benton, activities in the church, his own spiritual growth members of the church and the Benton circuit, and remembrances of revivals and protracted meetings. The diary entries vary in frequency from about one to five per year, recording his activities and opinions as a religious leader in the church, births and deaths of families and friends, quarterly meetings and district conferences attended, sermons heard, and prayers for his own spiritual growth. The last entry is dated February 22, 1878. An obituary noting Love-joy's death appears at the end of the volume.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Listed in the guide are items of note.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Peter Lovejoy diary, #6087. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Conversion and baptism; Rev. Abner Chase.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Revivals in Benton and on Benton Circuit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of preachers in the Benton charge.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page headed 1856-57 are listed Benton circuit preachers, 1825-1832.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Quarterly Meeting, presiding elder T.B. Hudson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two dreams or visions are recorded.
